NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JOHN DALTON AND GEORGE C. HANSEN'S MOTION FOR SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF YEVGENIY ZINCHIK JANUARY 29, 2010 COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE VERIFIED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS DEMONSTRATE THAT COMPLAINT IS BASED UPON PROTECTED ACTIVITY: THE PRIOR UNLAWFUL DETAINER LAWSUIT THAT WAS DISMISSED, THE SERVICE OF THE 3 DAY NOTICE, AND THE PROSECUTION OF THE CURRENT UNLAWFUL DETAINER LAWSUIT. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE THAT HE IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS JOHN DALTON AND GEORGE HANSEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 PER PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE FILED BY PLAINTIFF. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Defendants John Dalton And George C. Hansen'S Motion For Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff Yevgeniy Zinchik January 29, 2010 Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants John Dalton and George C. Hansen's Motion for Special Motion to Strike Plaintiff Yevgeniy Zinchik January 29, 2010 Complaint. Appearances: Oxana Kozlov, Esq. of IBCI Law Group, (650) 814-7708 specially appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein (415) 409-7611, appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike verified complaint is granted. Defendants demonstrate that complaint is based upon protected activity: the prior unlawful detainer lawsuit that was dismissed, the service of the 3 day notice, and the prosecution of the current unlawful detainer lawsuit. Plaintiff fails to present any evidence that he is likely to prevail on the merits.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
